description: Banned phrases for resume, cover letter, and LinkedIn content. Each entry includes replacement templates that require metrics or concrete actions.

returns.description: The exact matched text from the input
Replacement template. Placeholders: {N} = number, {%} = percentage, {$} = dollar amount, {VERB} = action verb, {OUTCOME} = concrete result
If true, the replacement MUST include a number or measurable outcome

banned_phrases.suggestion: Remove. Prove it with results: '{VERB} {OUTCOME}, achieving {N}% {metric}.'
Remove. Name the team and what you shipped together: 'Collaborated with {N}-person team to deliver {OUTCOME}.'
Remove. Show the passion with a concrete action: '{VERB} {OUTCOME} in {domain}.'
Remove. Prove it: 'Caught {N} production bugs in code review that prevented {OUTCOME}.'
Remove. Cite a concrete artifact: 'Wrote {OUTCOME} adopted by {N} teams.' or 'Presented {topic} to {N}-person exec audience.'
Remove. List the track: '{VERB} {N} {outcomes} over {time}, averaging {%} improvement.'
Remove entirely. Use a bullet that shows initiative: 'Identified and fixed {OUTCOME} without being asked, saving {N} hours/week.'
Remove. Show it: 'Independently scoped and shipped {OUTCOME} in {N} weeks.'
Replace with a plain verb: 'Used', 'Built with', 'Deployed', or 'Applied {technology} to {OUTCOME}.'
Delete. Write what actually happened: 'Partnered with {team} to {OUTCOME}.'
Replace with 'Led' or 'Owned', and add scope: 'Led {N}-person team to {OUTCOME} in {N} months.'
Keep only if followed by a measurable result: 'Streamlined {process}, cutting {N} hours/week.'
Remove. Cite output: 'Published {N} technical posts read by {N}+ engineers.' or 'Keynoted {conference}, {N} attendees.'
Delete. Quantify the value: '{VERB} {OUTCOME}, generating ${N} in savings / {N}% efficiency gain.'
Start with an action verb: 'Owned', 'Led', 'Built', 'Maintained', 'Reduced'. Never 'Responsible for'.
Claim your contribution clearly: 'Built the {component} that enabled {team} to {OUTCOME}.'
Specify your role: 'Designed the {system}', 'Wrote the {artifact}', 'Owned the {process}.'
Remove if used as a filler adjective. Replace with a specific trait backed by evidence.
Remove unless followed by a specific, novel approach: describe WHAT was new and WHY it hadn't been done before.
Replace with a number: '{N} years of {domain} experience' or cite specific depth with examples.
